## 233. `L1_P98__cast_remove__0`

- **算子**: `cast_remove` (Category C)
- **描述**: Remove explicit dtype casts (.to, .float, tl.cast, static_cast) @ L40
- **变异行**: Line 40, 原始片段 `static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx])`, 节点类型 `cast:cuda_static_cast`

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = pred_batch[idx];
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础输入生成代码** (所有压力策略在此基础上仅修改值分布，shape 不变)：

```python
batch_size = 128
input_shape = (4096, )
dim = 1

def get_inputs():
    return [torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1), torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1)]
```

**初始 3 组输入的 Tensor 规格 (seed=42/43/44)：**

```
seed=42:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.468e-06, 0.01472]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.578e-06, 0.0182]
seed=43:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.59e-06, 0.01239]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.393e-06, 0.01316]
seed=44:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[8.486e-07, 0.01363]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.633e-06, 0.01638]
```

**Layer 1 值分布压力测试 (14 策略 × 3 种子 = 42 次) 详细结果：**

| 策略 | 种子数 | 结果 | 描述 |
|------|--------|------|------|
| near_overflow | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e38 |
| large_magnitude | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e6 |
| near_zero | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-7 |
| denormals | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-40 (subnormal) |
| all_negative | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | all values < 0 |
| all_positive | ×3 | ORIG_ALSO_FAILS (原始也失败) | all values > 0 |
| mixed_extremes | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | mix of 1e6 and 1e-6 |
| alternating_sign | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | [+big, -big, +big, ...] |
| sparse | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | 90% zeros, 10% random |
| uniform_constant | ×3 | both_ok (未区分) | all elements = same constant |
| structured_ramp | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | linearly increasing 0→N |
| boundary_last_element | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last element set to extreme |
| head_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | first 10% large, rest small |
| tail_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last 10% large, rest small |

**Layer 2 (dtype 切换)**: 未杀死
**Layer 3 (重复执行)**: 一致
**Layer 4 (训练模式)**: 未杀死
**原始 kernel 也失败的策略**: all_positive

## 234. `L1_P98__cast_remove__1`

- **算子**: `cast_remove` (Category C)
- **描述**: Remove explicit dtype casts (.to, .float, tl.cast, static_cast) @ L41
- **变异行**: Line 41, 原始片段 `static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx])`, 节点类型 `cast:cuda_static_cast`

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = target_batch[idx];
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

**基础输入生成代码** (所有压力策略在此基础上仅修改值分布，shape 不变)：

```python
batch_size = 128
input_shape = (4096, )
dim = 1

def get_inputs():
    return [torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1), torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1)]
```

**初始 3 组输入的 Tensor 规格 (seed=42/43/44)：**

```
seed=42:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.468e-06, 0.01472]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.578e-06, 0.0182]
seed=43:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.59e-06, 0.01239]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.393e-06, 0.01316]
seed=44:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[8.486e-07, 0.01363]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.633e-06, 0.01638]
```

**Layer 1 值分布压力测试 (14 策略 × 3 种子 = 42 次) 详细结果：**

| 策略 | 种子数 | 结果 | 描述 |
|------|--------|------|------|
| near_overflow | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e38 |
| large_magnitude | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e6 |
| near_zero | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-7 |
| denormals | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-40 (subnormal) |
| all_negative | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | all values < 0 |
| all_positive | ×3 | ORIG_ALSO_FAILS (原始也失败) | all values > 0 |
| mixed_extremes | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | mix of 1e6 and 1e-6 |
| alternating_sign | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | [+big, -big, +big, ...] |
| sparse | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | 90% zeros, 10% random |
| uniform_constant | ×3 | both_ok (未区分) | all elements = same constant |
| structured_ramp | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | linearly increasing 0→N |
| boundary_last_element | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last element set to extreme |
| head_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | first 10% large, rest small |
| tail_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last 10% large, rest small |

**Layer 2 (dtype 切换)**: 未杀死
**Layer 3 (重复执行)**: 一致
**Layer 4 (训练模式)**: 未杀死
**原始 kernel 也失败的策略**: all_positive

## 235. `L1_P98__const_perturb__10`

- **算子**: `const_perturb` (Category A)
- **描述**: Perturb numeric literals (integer ±1; float ×1.01 or ×0.99) @ L138
- **变异行**: Line 138, 原始片段 `12`, 节点类型 `const:int+1`

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-13)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-11)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

| 策略 | 种子数 | 结果 | 描述 |
|------|--------|------|------|

| uniform_constant | ×3 | both_ok (未区分) | all elements = same constant |
| structured_ramp | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | linearly increasing 0→N |
| boundary_last_element | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last element set to extreme |
| head_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | first 10% large, rest small |
| tail_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last 10% large, rest small |

## 237. `L1_P98__epsilon_modify__1`

- **算子**: `epsilon_modify` (Category C)
- **描述**: Alter small epsilon literals (LayerNorm / safe_div / log stability) @ L138
- **变异行**: Line 138, 原始片段 `1e-12`, 节点类型 `eps:to_zero`

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=0)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx > batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础输入生成代码** (所有压力策略在此基础上仅修改值分布，shape 不变)：

```python
batch_size = 128
input_shape = (4096, )
dim = 1

def get_inputs():
    return [torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1), torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1)]
```

**初始 3 组输入的 Tensor 规格 (seed=42/43/44)：**

```
seed=42:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.468e-06, 0.01472]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.578e-06, 0.0182]
seed=43:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.59e-06, 0.01239]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.393e-06, 0.01316]
seed=44:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[8.486e-07, 0.01363]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.633e-06, 0.01638]
```

**Layer 1 值分布压力测试 (14 策略 × 3 种子 = 42 次) 详细结果：**

| 策略 | 种子数 | 结果 | 描述 |
|------|--------|------|------|
| boundary_last_element | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last element set to extreme |
| sparse | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | 90% zeros, 10% random |
| large_magnitude | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e6 |
| near_overflow | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e38 |
| near_zero | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-7 |
| denormals | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-40 (subnormal) |
| all_negative | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | all values < 0 |
| all_positive | ×3 | ORIG_ALSO_FAILS (原始也失败) | all values > 0 |
| mixed_extremes | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | mix of 1e6 and 1e-6 |
| alternating_sign | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | [+big, -big, +big, ...] |
| uniform_constant | ×3 | both_ok (未区分) | all elements = same constant |
| structured_ramp | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | linearly increasing 0→N |
| head_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | first 10% large, rest small |
| tail_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last 10% large, rest small |

**Layer 2 (dtype 切换)**: 未杀死
**Layer 3 (重复执行)**: 一致
**Layer 4 (训练模式)**: 未杀死
**原始 kernel 也失败的策略**: all_positive

## 239. `L1_P98__mask_boundary__1`

- **算子**: `mask_boundary` (Category B)
- **描述**: Weaken or tighten boundary checks in mask/tl.where (Triton) or thread/block guards (CUDA) @ L24
- **变异行**: Line 24, 原始片段 `batch_idx >= batch_size`, 节点类型 `ge_rhs+1`

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx >= batch_size + 1) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

## 240. `L1_P98__relop_replace__0`

- **算子**: `relop_replace` (Category A)
- **描述**: Replace relational operators (<→<=, <=→<, >→>=, >=→>, ==→!=, !=→==) @ L24
- **变异行**: Line 24, 原始片段 `>=`, 节点类型 `cuda_GtE`

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element

    if (batch_idx >= batch_size) return;

    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;

    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);

        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }

    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {

    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {

        }

        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {

            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for fused KL divergence
kl_div_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,
    int batch_size,
    int seq_len
) {
    // Each block processes one batch element
    int batch_idx = blockIdx.x;
    if (batch_idx > batch_size) return;
    
    const scalar_t* pred_batch = predictions + batch_idx * seq_len;
    const scalar_t* target_batch = targets + batch_idx * seq_len;
    
    // Thread block and warp setup
    cg::thread_block block = cg::this_thread_block();
    cg::thread_block_tile<32> warp = cg::tiled_partition<32>(block);
    
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    
    // Process elements with stride equal to total threads in block
    int tid = threadIdx.x;
    int total_threads = blockDim.x;
    
    for (int idx = tid; idx < seq_len; idx += total_threads) {
        float p = static_cast<float>(pred_batch[idx]);
        float t = static_cast<float>(target_batch[idx]);
        
        // KL divergence: t * log(t / p) = t * (log(t) - log(p))
        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div
        if (t > 0.0f && p > 0.0f) {
            thread_sum += t * (__logf(t) - __logf(p));
        }
        // Note: PyTorch's kl_div expects log(predictions) as input,
        // but we're computing the full KL divergence here
    }
    
    // Warp reduction
    float warp_sum = cg::reduce(warp, thread_sum, cg::plus<float>());
    
    // First thread in warp writes to shared memory
    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
        block_sums[warp.meta_group_rank()] = warp_sum;
    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;
        if (warp.thread_rank() < num_warps) {
            block_sum = block_sums[warp.thread_rank()];
        }
        
        float final_sum = cg::reduce(warp, block_sum, cg::plus<float>());
        
        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {
            // Store per-batch result
            output[batch_idx] = static_cast<scalar_t>(final_sum);
        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous
    predictions = predictions.contiguous();
    targets = targets.contiguous();
    
    auto batch_size = predictions.size(0);
    auto seq_len = predictions.size(1);
    
    // Create output tensor for per-batch results
    auto output = torch::zeros({batch_size}, predictions.options());
    
    // Optimized kernel launch configuration
    int threads = 256;
    int blocks = batch_size;
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES(predictions.scalar_type(), "fused_kl_div_kernel", ([&] {
        fused_kl_div_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads>>>(
            predictions.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            targets.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
            batch_size,
            seq_len
        );
    }));
    
    // Return batchmean reduction
    return output.mean();
}
"""

kl_div_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
fused_kl_div = load_inline(
    name="fused_kl_div",
    cpp_sources=kl_div_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=kl_div_source,
    functions=["fused_kl_div_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_cuda_cflags=["-O3", "--use_fast_math"],
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that computes Kullback-Leibler Divergence using fused CUDA kernels.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.fused_kl_div = fused_kl_div
        
    def forward(self, predictions, targets):
        # Input validation and preprocessing
        predictions = predictions.contiguous()
        targets = targets.contiguous()
        
        # Ensure numerical stability (matching PyTorch's behavior)
        predictions = torch.clamp(predictions, min=1e-12)
        targets = torch.clamp(targets, min=1e-12)
        
        # Normalize to probability distributions
        predictions = predictions / predictions.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        targets = targets / targets.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Use fused CUDA kernel
        return self.fused_kl_div.fused_kl_div_cuda(predictions, targets)
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础输入生成代码** (所有压力策略在此基础上仅修改值分布，shape 不变)：

```python
batch_size = 128
input_shape = (4096, )
dim = 1

def get_inputs():
    return [torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1), torch.randn(batch_size, *input_shape).softmax(dim=-1)]
```

**初始 3 组输入的 Tensor 规格 (seed=42/43/44)：**

```
seed=42:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.468e-06, 0.01472]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.578e-06, 0.0182]
seed=43:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.59e-06, 0.01239]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.393e-06, 0.01316]
seed=44:
  - inputs[0]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[8.486e-07, 0.01363]
  - inputs[1]: shape=[128, 4096], dtype=torch.float32, range=[1.633e-06, 0.01638]
```

**Layer 1 值分布压力测试 (14 策略 × 3 种子 = 42 次) 详细结果：**

| 策略 | 种子数 | 结果 | 描述 |
|------|--------|------|------|
| boundary_last_element | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last element set to extreme |
| structured_ramp | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | linearly increasing 0→N |
| large_magnitude | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e6 |
| near_overflow | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e38 |
| near_zero | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-7 |
| denormals | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | values ~1e-40 (subnormal) |
| all_negative | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | all values < 0 |
| all_positive | ×3 | ORIG_ALSO_FAILS (原始也失败) | all values > 0 |
| mixed_extremes | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | mix of 1e6 and 1e-6 |
| alternating_sign | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | [+big, -big, +big, ...] |
| sparse | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | 90% zeros, 10% random |
| uniform_constant | ×3 | both_ok (未区分) | all elements = same constant |
| head_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | first 10% large, rest small |
| tail_heavy | ×3 | REF_FAIL (参考实现崩溃) | last 10% large, rest small |

**Layer 2 (dtype 切换)**: 未杀死
**Layer 3 (重复执行)**: 一致
**Layer 4 (训练模式)**: 未杀死
**原始 kernel 也失败的策略**: all_positive

```python
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cooperative_groups.h>
#include <cooperative_groups/reduce.h>

namespace cg = cooperative_groups;

template<typename scalar_t>
__global__ void fused_kl_div_kernel(
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ predictions,
    const scalar_t* __restrict__ targets,
    scalar_t* __restrict__ output,

    int seq_len
) {

        // But we compute directly to match PyTorch's kl_div

        }

    }

    __shared__ float block_sums[32];
    if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {

    }
    block.sync();
    
    // First warp reduces all warp sums
    if (warp.meta_group_rank() == 0) {
        float block_sum = 0.0f;
        int num_warps = (total_threads + 31) / 32;

        }

        if (warp.thread_rank() == 0) {

        }
    }
}

torch::Tensor fused_kl_div_cuda(torch::Tensor predictions, torch::Tensor targets) {
    // Ensure inputs are on GPU and contiguous

    targets = targets.contiguous();

            output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),

}
"""

"""

    name="fused_kl_div",
```
